2603.18593.title: Language Model Maps for Prompt-Response Distributions via Log-Likelihood Vectors
2603.18593.paper_summary: We propose a method that represents language models by log-likelihood vectors over prompt-response pairs and constructs model maps for comparing their conditional distributions. In this space, distances between models approximate the KL divergence between the corresponding conditional distributions. Experiments on a large collection of publicly available language models show that the maps capture meaningful global structure, including relationships to model attributes and task performance. The method also captures systematic shifts induced by prompt modifications and their approximate additive compositionality, suggesting a way to analyze and predict the effects of composite prompt operations. We further introduce pointwise mutual information (PMI) vectors to reduce the influence of unconditional distributions; in some cases, PMI-based model maps better reflect training-data-related differences. Overall, the framework supports the analysis of input-dependent model behavior.
2603.18593.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一種方法，通過對提示-回應對的對數似然向量來表示語言模型，並構建模型地圖以比較它們的條件分佈。在這個空間中，模型之間的距離近似於相應條件分佈之間的KL散度。對大量公開可用的語言模型進行的實驗顯示，這些地圖捕捉到了有意義的全局結構，包括與模型屬性和任務表現的關係。該方法還捕捉到由提示修改引起的系統性變化及其近似的加法組合性，這表明了一種分析和預測複合提示操作影響的方法。我們進一步引入逐點互信息（PMI）向量，以減少無條件分佈的影響；在某些情況下，基於PMI的模型地圖更好地反映了與訓練數據相關的差異。總體而言，該框架支持對輸入依賴模型行為的分析。
2603.18593.author: Yusuke Takase et.al.
2603.18593.authors: Yusuke Takase, Momose Oyama, Hidetoshi Shimodaira
